English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Aaron Cometbus Punk and Underground Press Collection
Cometbus, Aaron, 1968-
8107
14.9 cubic feet. 14.9 cubic feet.
ca. 1977-2018. Collection material in English Flyers, posters, magazines, files, and zines relating to American punk and alternative culture collected or created by Aaron Cometbus, including his own work as a writer and musician. Flyers, printed material, posters, photographs, correspondence, and ephemera. 9250664
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Aaron Elliott, better known as Aaron Cometbus, has published Cometbus magazine since 1981. He is also an accomplished novelist, lyricist, and drummer.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Flyers, posters, magazines, files, and zines relating to American punk and alternative culture collected or created by Aaron Cometbus, including his own work as a writer and musician. The archive contains hundreds of fanzines and small magazines, plus original manuscripts and paste-ups for Cometbus and related publishing and distribution records. Board games, business cards, set lists, and venue histories provide a personal, behind-the-scenes view of punk both as music and as a movement, from 1980 to the present.
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
January 2017
EAD encoding:
Marcie Farwell, December 2016
Date modified:
Ben Wrubel, October 2018
NOTES
Introduction to the collection written by Aaron Elliot can be found in Box 1, Folder A.
Cite As:
Aaron Cometbus Punk and Underground Press Collection, #8107. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Punk and Underground Press Materials
a. Bay Area 1. Titles A-O 2. Titles P-Z
b. Other Locations 1. Titles A-D 2. Titles E-O 3. Titles P-Z
c. Underground Newspapers 1. 1960s Underground Newspapers 2. High School Underground Newspapers
d. Other Zine Material
e. Cometbus and Related Materials 1. Cometbus Issues 2. Cometbus Drafts and Proofs 3. Cometbus Files
Series II. Event Flyers and Posters
a. Flyers 1. Assorted 2. Shows: Bay Area 3. Shows: Other Locations 4. Other Types of Flyers
b. Posters
Series III. Political Materials
Series IV. Venue Materials
Series V. Band Files and Related Materials
Series VI. Ephemera
a. Photographs b. Assorted Ephemera c. Board Games
Series VII. Sound Recordings
Series VIII. Letters to Cometbus Magazine
Conditions Governing Use
Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage.
Conditions Governing Use
Audio visual media that has been digitized: TR-15991.

Scope and Contents
Includes fanzines, magazines, newsletters, comics, and newspapers
Arrangement
The first two subseries are arranged geographically starting with locations in the Bay Area and then alphabetically by title within each location.
Processing Information
"Creator" is used throughout Series I to denote the author, editor, or publisher of a work.Bay Area1977-2016

Scope and Contents
Two issues. Donor note: This fanzine shouldn't be taken as a representative example of the form, the scene, or the time. "Punkette" was an alien phrase then as now, and what made this mag interesting was how totally off the editor seemed, perhaps a 45-year old shut-in reporting on a youth culture that excited him but which he knew little about. Sort of a precursor to the internet!San Jose, CaliforniaFilthCollison, RobDonohoe, JoeSperry, ChuckWylie, Daniel1996-06713

Scope and Contents
Two issues. Donor note: This fanzine shouldn't be taken as a representative example of the form, the scene, or the time. "Punkette" was an alien phrase then as now, and what made this mag interesting was how totally off the editor seemed, perhaps a 45-year old shut-in reporting on a youth culture that excited him but which he knew little about. Sort of a precursor to the internet!San Jose, CaliforniaFilthCollison, RobDonohoe, JoeSperry, ChuckWylie, Daniel1996-06713
